_comment: PHASE 2 — reusable company environments, shared by EVERY career. A career references these by id in its `supportedCompanyTypes`. Validated by companyTypeSchema. The `modifiers` make the same task feel different per environment without any career-specific code.

companyTypes.name: Startup
companyTypes.description: A small, fast-moving company where you wear many hats and ship every day.
companyTypes.culture: Informal, high ownership, move-fast-and-learn. Everyone talks to everyone.
companyTypes.workStyle: Hands-on, broad scope, minimal process, direct impact on the product.
responsibilities: Jump across the stack or across roles when needed
Talk directly to users and founders
companyTypes.communicationStyle: Rapid, informal chat. Decisions happen in threads, not documents.
companyTypes.growthSpeed: Very fast — responsibility grows faster than the title.

companyTypes.name: Medium Company
companyTypes.description: An established company with defined roles, real process, and cross-team work.
companyTypes.culture: Balanced. Clear ownership, collaborative, room to specialise.
companyTypes.workStyle: Focused role, structured sprints, coordinated work across teams.
responsibilities: Follow the team's process and standards
companyTypes.communicationStyle: Mix of chat and scheduled meetings. Decisions are documented.
companyTypes.growthSpeed: Steady — clear paths and regular review cycles.

companyTypes.name: Enterprise / MNC
companyTypes.description: A large multinational with layers of structure, compliance, and scale.
companyTypes.culture: Formal, process-driven, specialised. Stability and quality over speed.
companyTypes.workStyle: Deep specialisation, formal approvals, large-scale systems, documentation-heavy.
responsibilities: Deliver a well-defined slice of a large system
Follow compliance, security, and review gates
Coordinate across departments and time zones
companyTypes.communicationStyle: Formal. Meetings, tickets, and sign-offs. Decisions require approval.
companyTypes.growthSpeed: Structured and slower — promotions follow defined criteria and cycles.
